channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Farm equipment and electric fence blocking public right of way, 8th March
item.description:
Lots of farm equipment with an electric fence all around it is blocking the public right of way here. No signage and unkept path through the firld

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cotswold
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lorries / buses driving on grass verge since changes to double yellow lines, 8th March
item.description:
Grass verge outside 91 Gretton Road Since changes to double yellow lines cars have parked further up Gretton Road. Now lorries / buses coming up Gretton Road on the wrong side seem to be driving on grass verge to pass waiting queue of oncoming cars. The road is narrow here. Could the double yellow lines be extended to help avoid this?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Gretton Road, Winchcombe, Winchcombe
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 3 large potholes outside my house on number 143 Hatherley Rd. This is incredibly unsafe every time a huge Bosco’s past my house and hits the pothole my entire house shakes. I am scared a car will lose its wheel or wheel trim resulting in the potential of my property / family being hit by flying objects. If a car loses control because it is hit one of these large potholes there is also the danger they will swerve off the road and potentially knock my family over who may be getting into their car via our drive. This is serious and needs sorting ASAP., 8th March
item.description:
Outside no 143 Hatherley Road… i’m concerned for the personal safety of my family when getting in our car on our open drive and of my property being hit through wheel trims coming off wheels, tyres blowing and cars swerving…. due to these multiple large hot holes.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 159, Hatherley Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Potholes and broken road surface, 8th March
item.description:
Road is in a dangerous state due to the broken surface and potholes. My car was damaged in February and the road still hasn't been repaired to a safe standard. GCC are bring negligent in not repairing this road properly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckhampton Hill, Shurdington, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road closed sign on Andover Roac, 8th March
item.description:
There is a Severn Trent road closed sign on Andover Road, close to junction with Park Place which is out of date (says 23-25 Feb)

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Andover Road, Tivoli, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pothole just outside of Oldwell Cottage driveway, 7th March
item.description:
Just outside of Oldwell Cottage driveway

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Blacksmiths Lane, Leigh, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside Tesco, Queen's Road, Cheltenham, 7th March
item.description:
I would like an answer to the question: Why does GCC use such a poor specification for the repair of potholes and resurfacing of trenches? Other contractors use a far better spec which lasts. There was an enormous pothole in the bus layby outside Tesco in Queen’s Road. One Sunday a few years ago, Atkins came and repaired it and installed a gully. This never drained properly, so a gang dug trenches to the gully, which then worked. However, it was obvious the trench surfacing would not last, and sure enough another gang came and did repairs to one of the trenches (again, poor quality). That’s 3 visits so far to repair one problem, and potholes are again appearing in the trench surface. If you’d done the job properly, you could have been getting on with other jobs. It's the same with a pothole at the rear of the station, in the toucan crossing in Gloucester Road. The pothole was repaired in 2019, 2020 and 2021. It is holding so far, but won’t last many more years, whereas other non-GCC holes and trenches in the vicinity are holding up extremely well. So – Why does GCC use such a poor spec for its repairs?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Queen's Road, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Massive potholes, 7th March
item.description:
These potholes have been there for over 2 weeks, and is just getting worse. Is impossible not to go thru them. This is an emergency as cars will be damaged.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Hayden Road, Hesters Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
